texto: There were so many things Sebastian and I had to work out: we'd both been single for so long that blending our lives together wasn't going to be easy.I'd promised Sebastian we'd find a way. He deserved to be loved for everything he was. And for whatever crazy reason he had, he loved me, too.
explicacao: Citação de Jane Harvey-Berrick sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “There were so many things Sebastian and I had to work out: we'd both been single for so long that…” articula amor, love-story, romance em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como termino e trabalho.

frase_original: There were so many things Sebastian and I had to work out: we'd both been single for so long that blending our lives together wasn't going to be easy.I'd promised Sebastian we'd find a way. He deserved to be loved for everything he was. And for whatever crazy reason he had, he loved me, too.

texto: It didn't matter what I wore, or how much sleep I'd had or not had, he always looked at me as if his world began and ended with me.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jane Harvey-Berrick: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “It didn't matter what I wore, or how much sleep I'd had or not had, he always looked at me as if …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, sweet, matter pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e reflexao.
